---
title: "Government & Public Finances"
title-block-banner: "#17324D"
title-block-banner-color: "#F7F4EE"
body-classes: area-government
---

![](../visual_assets/brand/botanical/solid/leaf-display-navy.svg){.overview-leaf fig-alt="The Canada Observatory leaf in the Government & Public Finances colour"}

What governments owe and spend, who works for them, and how Canada's public finances compare with a broad group of OECD peers. Three views:

::::{.columns}

:::{.column .area-card .area-government width="32%"}
![](../visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-government.svg){.area-banner}

### [Government Finances](index.qmd)
Debt, deficits, revenue, the tax mix, interest costs, and defence — Canada's
general-government finances against 17 OECD peers.
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column .area-card .area-government width="32%"}
![](../visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-government.svg){.area-banner}

### [Government Employment](../government/index.qmd)
Who works for government — employment by level, the whole public-sector
workforce, the federal public service, and an OECD peer comparison.
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column .area-card .area-government width="32%"}
![](../visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-government.svg){.area-banner}

### [Federal Spending](../government/spending.qmd)
Federal revenue and spending back to 1961 — by economic type, standard object,
department, and function.
:::

::::
